methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.rationale: Median across 4 same-sector + same/adjacent-stage peers in Defense & Security. Range: $60M - $500M.

public.rationale: Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use
Public-co frontier-tech comp in Defense & Dual-use

private.name: Tenna Systems
private.rationale: Same sector (Defense & Security), same stage (Seed), post-money $60M

data.narrative:
Volund Manufacturing is a seed-stage company in Defense & Security founded by Eric Hostetler. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 4 same-sector, same/adjacent-stage peers, supplemented by a broader set of 10 comparables (2 public, 8 private) that includes companies such as Tenna Systems, Anduril Industries, and Scale AI.

The peer-median method applies directly to this target. The four stage-aligned comparables—Stark Defence ($500M Series A), Vatn Systems ($300M Series A), Ares Industries ($150M Series A), and Tenna Systems ($60M Seed)—yield a median valuation of $225M, with a range spanning $60M to $500M. This peer-median result serves as the sole triangulated anchor given data availability constraints.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $191M to $259M, centered on $225M. This median estimate reflects the comparable peer set, though material uncertainty persists around stage discount relative to Series A cohort members and Volund's relative positioning within the seed-stage subset. The tight comparables universe (n=4) and reliance on a single method limit the robustness of this estimate. Investors should weight execution risk, technical differentiation, and market traction as primary refinement factors when determining actual entry price.
